title: [FIRRTL] Raw Param Difference vs. SFC
body:
Currently, CIRCT will squash "raw" FIRRTL external module parameters into string parameters. This produces a difference with the SFC. Consider:

```
circuit Foo:
  extmodule Bar:
    output a: UInt<1>
    parameter foo = 'hello'
    parameter bar = "world"

  module Foo:
    output a: UInt<1>

    inst bar of Bar
    a <= bar.a
```

This compiles with CIRCT to:

```verilog
module Foo(
  output a);

  Bar #(
    .foo("hello"),
    .bar("world")
  ) bar (
    .a (a)
  );
endmodule
```

With SFC, this produces:

```verilog
module Foo(
  output  a
);
  wire  bar_a;
  Bar #(.foo(hello), .bar("world")) bar (
    .a(bar_a)
  );
  assign a = bar_a;
endmodule
```
